data.title: XRP Currently Offers a Strong Risk:Reward Setup as Downside Fuel Nearly Gone
data.description:
The current XRP position could offer one of the strongest risk:reward setups as downside fuel continues to deplete. XRP has witnessed one of its longest stretches of downward price action, having recorded five consecutive monthly red candlesticks, a bearish development that last played out in late 2016 to early 2017. Visit Website
source: https://thecryptobasic.com/2026/03/07/xrp-currently-offers-a-strong-riskreward-setup-as-downside-fuel-nearly-gone/

data.title: Another robbery involving millions of dollars in cash has occurred at a money exchange shop in Hong Kong, marking the third such incident in the past three months.
data.description: Yesterday morning, two victims were robbed at knifepoint inside the Sheung Wan MTR station after exchanging approximately HK$10 million at a currency exchange shop in Sheung Wan, Hong Kong. However, thanks to prior police intervention, the robbers were apprehended and the victims suffered no financial loss. In the past three months, three large-scale cash robberies have occurred in the bustling Sheung Wan district of Hong Kong, with the victims all being customers of currency exchange shops. Previously, on December 18, 2025, and January 30, two similar large-scale cash robberies involving cryptocurrency exchange shops occurred. It is understood that Hong Kong police have noticed the three robberies related to foreign exchange shops in recent months. In response to these cases, the police have adopted intelligence-led operations, utilizing additional surveillance cameras under "Operation Sharp Eye," and successfully collected crucial evidence. The police will conduct a thorough investigation to determine if other members or criminal groups were behind the operation, and whether this case is related to the previous Sheung Wan robberies. Simultaneously, the police will focus on investigating how the robbers knew the victims would be exchanging large sums of money and whether they had scouted the location beforehand. Currently, there is no evidence linking the victims to the arrested individuals. (Caixin.com)

data.title: Large XRP Holders Have Been Pulling Coins Off Binance
data.description:
Two significant whale outflow events from Binance occurred within the same month, according to CryptoQuant data compiled by analyst Amr Taha. On February 27, whale wallets recorded a net outflow of approximately 44 million XRP from Binance, one of the largest single-day withdrawal events visible on the chart.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/large-xrp-holders-have-been-pulling-coins-off-binance/

data.title: Dubai gold is trading at a rare discount, possibly due to logistical disruptions caused by conflict in the Middle East.
data.description: With flights grounded and logistics disrupted due to the Middle East conflict, large quantities of gold stranded in Dubai are being sold at significant discounts. Sources familiar with the matter revealed that many buyers have suspended new purchase orders due to exceptionally high transportation and insurance costs and the inability to guarantee timely delivery. As a result, under pressure from indefinitely incurring storage and financing costs, traders have begun selling gold at discounts of up to $30 per ounce below the London benchmark price. Sources said that although some gold shipments were loaded onto flights departing Dubai starting in the middle of the week, many shipments remained stranded as of Friday. The UAE, especially Dubai, is a major global gold refining and trading center, exporting gold not only to various parts of Asia but also serving as a crucial transit hub for gold from Switzerland, the UK, and several African countries. Due to the escalating conflict in the Middle East, parts of the UAE's airspace have been closed; meanwhile, the US-Israel military strikes against Iran have entered their seventh day, with no end in sight. (Cailian Press)

data.title: Explosions were heard at Dubai International Airport, and an interception operation was launched overhead.
data.description: Explosions were heard at Dubai International Airport in the United Arab Emirates on the morning of the 7th local time, and some passengers in the terminal were asked to evacuate to the basement for safety. It is reported that an interception operation was carried out over Dubai Airport. The Iranian military announced in its 15th statement today that from early that morning until recently, its naval forces conducted a large-scale drone strike against US military concentrations and bases near Abu Dhabi Airport in the UAE, Camp Ali Jain in Kuwait, and Israeli radar. (CCTV)

data.title: Bybit co-CEO Helen Liu will step down to start her own business.
data.description: According to community news, Bybit announced that Co-CEO Helen Liu will step down on April 30, 2026, to start her own business. Helen Liu joined Bybit in 2020, where she served as Vice President of Human Resources, Vice President of Marketing, Chief of Staff, and Chief Operating Officer (COO), before becoming Co-CEO in 2025.

data.description: 1. The People's Bank of China increases its gold reserves for the 16th consecutive month; 2. Iran launches missile at the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier; 3. Bloomberg analyst: The US CFTC may share offices with the SEC, but the two will not merge; 4. Bloomberg analyst: BlackRock applies to adjust Ethereum ETF staking fees, potentially offering tiered discounts; 6. A whale deposits 2.18 million USDC into Hyperliquid to open a 10x leveraged short position in ETH; 9. Coinbase Prime launches a unified margin function, covering spot, derivatives, and regulated perpetual contracts; 10. The US CFTC officially updates its logo, stating it will usher in a "golden age" of innovation; 11. Continue Capital was liquidated on Hyperliquid with 10,000 SNDK and 22,300 MU, worth $13.3 million; 12. Robinhood's IPO on the NYSE closed down 16% on its first day of trading, raising less than its target; 13. SOL Treasury's DeFi Development plans to launch a dividend-paying stablecoin; 14. The U.S. General Services Administration has terminated the OneGov agreement with Anthropic; 15. A New York federal judge granted investors in the Tether vs. Bitfinex market manipulation case standing to file a class-action lawsuit.

data.title: India Directs Refineries to Boost LPG Production Amid Emergency Powers
data.description: India has invoked emergency powers to instruct its refineries to increase the production of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG). According to Jin10, this move comes as the country seeks to address domestic energy needs and ensure sufficient supply for its population. The decision reflects India's proactive approach to managing energy resources and highlights the government's commitment to maintaining stability in the energy sector. The directive is expected to impact the operations of refineries across the nation, as they adjust their output to meet the new requirements. This development underscores the importance of LPG in India's energy strategy and the government's focus on energy security.

data.title: Rumble Reports Bitcoin Holdings and Strategic Partnership with Tether
data.description: Rumble, a company listed on Nasdaq, has disclosed its financial status in its latest annual report. According to PANews, the company currently holds 210.82 bitcoins and possesses cash and cash equivalents valued at $237.9 million, bringing its total liquidity to approximately $256.4 million. Additionally, Rumble revealed that stablecoin issuer Tether has committed to a $100 million advertising investment, to be paid over two years at $50 million annually, with the first installment due in the first quarter of this year. Tether has also pledged up to $150 million in GPU service procurement over the next two years, aiming to enhance strategic collaboration to expand AI infrastructure and high-performance computing capabilities.

data.title: Understanding Market Cycles in Cryptocurrency Trading
data.description: CyrilXBT posted on X, highlighting a straightforward rule to comprehend the market cycle in cryptocurrency trading. The cycle begins with Bitcoin (BTC) making initial moves, followed by Ethereum (ETH). Subsequently, large-cap cryptocurrencies experience growth, leading to mid-cap cryptocurrencies surging, and finally, low-cap cryptocurrencies witnessing significant increases. This sequence eventually leads to a market reset. Understanding this rotation is crucial for traders to strategically position themselves in the market.

data.title: Solana Rally Over? SOL Risks 2022-Like Correction As Price Erases Mid-Week Recovery
data.description:
As the broader crypto market retraces, Solana (SOL) has erased its recent gains despite strong institutional demand for investment products based on the cryptocurrency. Some analysts have now suggested that the altcoin risks a deeper pullback similar to its 2022 correction. Related Reading: Ethereum ETFs Record Best Single-Day Performance Since January With $169M Inflows Solana Loses Mid-Week Gains As Market Wobbles On Friday, Solana dropped 7% intraday to retest the $84 area again, retracing most of its intraweek gains. The cryptocurrency had been trading between $78-$88 since the early February crash, attempting to break out of its local range but ultimately failing
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/solana-risks-2022-correction-price-erases-recovery/

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $349 million yesterday, with none of the twelve ETFs experiencing net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $349 million yesterday (March 6th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was Fidelity ETF (FBTC), with a net outflow of $159 million. FBTC's historical total net outflow has reached $153 million. This was followed by BlackRock ETF (IBIT), with a single-day net outflow of $143 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow has reached $660 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $87.075 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.39%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $55.369 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $82.8519 million yesterday, with none of the nine ETFs experiencing net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $82.8519 million yesterday (March 6th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the Fidelity ETF (FETH), with a net outflow of $67.5669 million. FETH's historical total net outflow has reached $218 million. This was followed by the Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF (ETH), with a single-day net outflow of $5.9979 million. ETH's historical total net inflow has reached $84.1805 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $11.283 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.72%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.629 billion.
